message:
test(mcp-conformance): canonicalize wa.wait_for polls in goldens (ft-55l4f)

wa_wait_for.json froze polls:1, but the scheduler can issue a second
poll before the first fake-wezterm.py subprocess returns — under swarm
CI load where many cargo test runs compete for CPU/disk, the recorded
polls jumps past 1 and the byte-stable golden fails.

Option 2 from the bead: keep the semantic invariant (polls >= 1) in
assert_wait_for_success_data at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:474-477
(runs against the live envelope BEFORE canonicalization at :733), and
add `polls` to the canonicalize allowlist alongside now/elapsed_ms/
captured_at so the golden only freezes structural presence.

Only wa_wait_for.json carries a `polls` key under goldens/, so the
rule is unambiguous.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-1p752","title":"[testing-fuzzing] crash-detector fuzz target for frankenterm-escape-parser Parser::parse raw bytes","description":"# Motivation

`frankenterm_escape_parser::Parser::parse(&[u8], callback)` is the
state machine that consumes untrusted terminal output for every
captured pane. The crate has 2 existing fuzz targets:

  * `termwiz_csi_parser.rs` — structure-aware around CSI sequences
  * `osc_marker_parser.rs`  — structure-aware around OSC markers

Neither is a plain raw-bytes crash detector. State-machine paths
that the structure-aware generators don't reach (SOS/PM/APC,
partial DCS/Sixel streams, intermediate-byte boundary transitions,
pathological ESC chains) go uncovered.

# Scope

New fuzz target `fuzz/fuzz_targets/escape_parser_raw.rs` that feeds
arbitrary bytes into `Parser::parse` with a no-op callback, asserts
no panic / no infinite loop / no allocator blow-up. Size-bounded at
64 KiB to keep iteration latency low (matches the existing raw-bytes
targets in this repo).

# Invariants (beyond crash-freedom)

1. `parse` consumes all bytes it's fed (state machine never strands).
2. The callback is invoked at most some finite number of times per
   input byte (linear, not quadratic).
3. `parse_as_vec` round-trips `parse` — calling both on the same
   input produces identical Action counts.

# Seed corpus

Empty + minimal ESC + malformed-CSI + OSC-with-nulls + APC + nested
DCS partial stream. 4-5 seeds.

# Build

    cargo +nightly fuzz run escape_parser_raw -- -runs=100000

Targets 2000+ exec/s given escape parsing is hot-path byte-driven

 {"id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","title":"Publish user-facing release/tuning guidance for different hardware tiers","description":"Provide clear guidance on expected behavior, config knobs, and troubleshooting paths across low/mid/high-end hardware.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Publish hardware-tier guidance backed by validated unit/integration/e2e/perf evidence from the resize/reflow program.
2. Guidance must include explicit operational playbooks, rollback/fallback steps, and diagnostics workflows for each tier.
3. Require structured logging/diagnostics artifact references so operators can map symptoms to recommended actions quickly.
4. Validate guidance via scripted end-to-end operator journeys and update docs when discrepancies are discovered.
5. Added cross-link from docs/resize-rollout-plan-wa-1u90p.8.2.md so rollout documentation chain now references this guidance.","created_at":"2026-02-20T01:55:29Z"},{"id":2624,"issue_id":"ft-1u90p.8.5","author":"CobaltCanyon","text":"CobaltCanyon starting a bounded docs/ops slice. -{"id":"ft-1xkh9","title":"[patterns] builtin:core default pack is an empty placeholder","description":"File: crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:1442
Current state: builtin_core_pack() is documented as the shared/core built-in pack but returns PatternPack::new("builtin:core", "0.1.0", Vec::new()).
Impact: config/environment surfaces default to builtin:core, so config-driven PatternEngine construction can silently load zero default detection rules.
Should replace: builtin:core should materialize the shipped default rule set (at minimum the current built-in Codex/Claude Code/Gemini/WezTerm rules) rather than exposing an empty placeholder pack.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:20.454161Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:26.771753Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-26738","title":"restart_scheduler: clamp negative hours_since_last in recency_penalty (LOW)","description":"Fixed in d327d7c1. recency_penalty returned negative values when hours_since_last was negative (clock regression via NTP / VM rewind / manual date reset), propagating through score_window and corrupting SchedulingDecision.windows ranking. Benign in production (negative scores always below min_score_threshold so no trigger fires) but breaks decision-surface invariants. Fix clamps to 0 (treat as just restarted). 2 regression tests pin the invariant. Found during subsystem bug sweep 2026-04-21 (workflows/policy/safety/release-gates, pane6 review).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:27.355801Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:30.892645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:30.892323Z","close_reason":"Fixed in d327d7c1","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-26k3h","title":"[MEDIUM] survival::ActivityProfile::update_hour NaN-poisons hourly_ewma via clamp","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:455` — `ActivityProfile::update_hour`.

Same `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` gotcha as ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce. A NaN `normalized_activity`:

1. `sample = normalized_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` stays NaN.
2. First sample (sample_count == 0) path writes `self.hourly_ewma[index] = NaN`.
3. Any subsequent EWMA update is `alpha * NaN + (1 - alpha) * prev = NaN`.
4. Every `predict_hour` / `predict` call returns NaN for that hour slot, forever.
5. Downstream survival/hazard math using the predicted activity gets NaN — same silent-wrong-answer class as disk_pressure.

## Fix

Guard at entry: if `normalized_activity.is_nan()`, skip the update entirely. The slot keeps its last good value (or the default if uninitialised) and the next well-formed sample continues the EWMA.

## Tests

* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_does_not_poison_ewma` — update_hour(NaN) leaves hourly_ewma + sample_count unchanged.
* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_then_clean_sample_still_tracks` — end-to-end: NaN → clean sample sequence still produces a finite, sensible EWMA value.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:50:37.797018Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:53:29.312035Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:53:29.311735Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2guux","title":"reality-check gaps","description":"Reality-check sweep of the repo (pane 5, session 2026-04-20) against
README.md + AGENTS.md + docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md. Most
capability claims match the code; the four material gaps below are
worth tracking. No fixes this tick — documentation-only finding.

Methodology: read the README "Supported Surface Matrix" and "Quick
Example", then grep the CLI command tree at `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`,
the MCP tools at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs`, the web
endpoints at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/router.rs`, and the
runtime layer at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs` +
`runtime_compat.rs`.

Findings (severity ranked):

G1 [MEDIUM] Watch-daemon asupersync claim vs actual reactor dependency
  Evidence:
    - README.md:38-41 "runtime model is asupersync-native",
      "runtime_compat remains as an audited boundary".
    - crates/frankenterm-core/tests/runtime_labruntime.rs:13-18
      explicit comment:
        "Full ObservationRuntime integration tests (startup,
         shutdown, discovery, capture) require the runtime_compat::task
         module to be migrated from tokio::spawn to asupersync::spawn.
         Until that migration completes, ObservationRuntime::start()
         cannot run under asupersync RuntimeFixture because task::spawn
         → tokio::spawn panics without a tokio reactor."
    - runtime.rs:184 uses `crate::runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx`
      which still goes through the tokio-backed path when the
      `asupersync-runtime` feature is disabled.
  Why it matters: the primary operator surface (`ft watch`) cannot be
  exercised end-to-end under a pure asupersync runtime fixture today.
  The README is honest that runtime_compat is "an audited boundary";
  the specific gap is that the watch daemon's task spawns transit
  that boundary. Tracked by the ft-xbnl0.2.x epic but worth surfacing
  as a doc clarification on README.md:38-41 ("native with a named
  quarantine").

G2 [MEDIUM] `ft restart` is Unix-only, not disclosed in the support matrix
  Evidence:
    - README.md:124 "Session persistence | Supported with backend
      prerequisite | Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and
      `ft session doctor` are implemented".
    - crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27032 emits:
        "ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms."
      and exits 1 on non-Unix; the full `execute_restart_workflow`
      call path sits inside `#[cfg(unix)]` at line 27036.
  Why it matters: a Windows operator following the README's
  session-persistence story will hit this only at runtime.
  Supportable via an asterisk in the matrix or an explicit
  "Unix-only" caveat in the `ft restart` section.

G3 [LOW] Performance claims lack linked artifacts
  Evidence:
    - README.md:51 "<50ms lag" for delta extraction.
    - README.md:62 "keeps 200+ panes under 1GB vs 4GB+".
    - README.md:113 "TOON reduces token consumption by 40-60%".
    - No matching benchmark artifact path in docs/ referenced from
      these claims; the verification-contract doc at docs/ft-xbnl0-
      verification-contract.md mandates Level C artifact bundles for
      soak/perf-adjacent claims but the README does not cite any.
  Why it matters: performance claims presented as current fact in
  the README should either cite an artifact bundle or be softened to
  "target" / "design goal" until evidence lands. Non-blocking.

G4 [LOW] `SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT = 200` silently bounds OSC133 state
  Evidence:
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:188
        `const SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT: usize = 200;`
    - mcp.rs:288-310 `derive_osc_state_from_storage` walks only the
      last 200 segments. If OSC133 markers span further back, the
      derived state is silently incomplete; the helper returns
      `Ok(None)` with no gap marker.
  Why it matters: a quiet-pane edge case (no activity for >200
  segments) would yield an incorrect alt-screen / prompt state via
  the MCP surface without a visible warning. Worth either documenting
  the bound or recording a `markers_unknown_due_to_window_limit`
  signal.

Non-gaps verified present and matching claims:

  - `ft robot state` / `state --include-text`: main.rs:2375-2391
  - `ft robot search --mode {lexical,semantic,hybrid}`:
      main.rs:4633-4711 enum + From impl; semantic/hybrid properly
      gated behind `semantic-search`/`frankensearch` features.
  - `ft mcp serve`: main.rs:33124
  - `ft web` + `/stream/events` + `/stream/deltas`:
      web/router.rs:65,70 — streaming exists; README's
      "more than 'health only'" claim is accurate.
  - `ft distributed agent`: main.rs:165-168 under
      `#[cfg(feature = "distributed")]`.
  - `ft session doctor`, snapshot save/restore/list/inspect/diff:
      SnapshotCommands + SessionCommands enums defined around
      main.rs:4207.
  - `ft tx run`/`tx rollback`/`tx plan`/`tx show`: main.rs:21114+
      via `execute_tx_run_with_executor`.
  - Policy "21 subsystems": unified_telemetry.rs:242 comment; actual
      PolicyEngine struct at policy.rs:3466-3517 declares ~22
      subsystem-shaped fields. Close-enough; no drift worth fixing.
  - tokio/smol usage in supported core: grep finds zero production
      call sites outside `runtime_compat` + `dependency_eradication`
      + `forbidden_dep_guards` (guard files are intentional).

Documented only. No shippable fix this tick per /reality-check-for-project
ground rules. Related: AGENTS.md rule zero (NEVER delete
frankenterm-core) is intact; the four gaps above are additions to the
README, not subtractions from the code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:01:41.726765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.915672Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.913552Z","close_reason":"Reopening-in-error by p5-orch robot-triage pick — the bead was already closed with the four reality-check gaps (G1 runtime_compat quarantine language, G2 ft restart Unix-only caveat, G3 softened perf/token claims, G4 OSC/OSC133 marker window bound) addressed across two commits:

1. 4c3952a9 docs: correct README claims vs actual (ft-2guux) — landed 2026-04-20, covers all four gaps:
   - G1: added 'The remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses a named runtime_compat quarantine while the last task-spawn seams are being collapsed' to the Platform Model paragraph
   - G2: appended 'ft restart is currently Unix-only.' to the Session persistence row of the support matrix
   - G3: softened <50ms lag to 'target/benchmark lane'; softened 200+ pane memory envelope to 'benchmark-dependent until linked artifacts are published'; softened 40-60% TOON savings to 'lower-token machine format; savings vary by payload'
   - G4: added a new subsection '5.1 Marker Window Bound' documenting the bounded OSC/OSC133 marker derivation window

2. af925d26 docs(readme): correct runtime_compat boundary claim — landed 2026-04-21, further refined G1 language now that the 9e1bd63b cfg-strip has collapsed the dual-runtime fallback: 'The dual-runtime fallback is gone, but several production seams still route through the named runtime_compat boundary for runtime bootstrap, channels, time, blocking work, IPC, and mux interoperability while the remaining compat helpers are being collapsed.'

bv --robot-triage surfaced this bead as open-and-ready because the triage graph snapshot had not yet absorbed the prior close. Verified via git log --grep ft-2guux + direct README diff inspection: no work remains. Restoring closed status.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","docs","readme","reality-check"]}

 {"id":"ft-4o5mb.3","title":"[conformance] wa.reserve / wa.reservations / wa.release lack envelope coverage","description":"Add a dedicated MCP conformance test file for the reservation tool family. Use the mission/tx golden-capture pattern, seed storage directly, and pin success plus invalid-args envelopes for wa.reserve, wa.reservations, and wa.release without touching mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:36:28.848013Z","created_by":"LilacBay","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:36:28.848013Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.3","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:36:28.848013Z","created_by":"LilacBay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4z7vh","title":"RobotCommands::Workflow builds PolicyEngine without require_prompt_active/command_gate/rules — bypasses operator safety config","description":"AUTH BYPASS (robot mode mutations, Severity: Medium).

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs has 10 PolicyEngine::new call sites. 8 of them wire require_prompt_active from config + .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()). Two do NOT, both under RobotCommands::Workflow:

Site 1 (main.rs:19831, RobotCommands::Workflow::Run):

  let policy_engine = PolicyEngine::new(
      config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane,
      config.safety.rate_limit_global,
      false, // Don't require prompt active for robot mode
  )
  .with_tuning(&config.tuning);

No command_gate, no policy_rules, and require_prompt_active hardcoded false. This engine is fed into CxPolicyInjector → WorkflowRunner; any workflow step that calls send_text (e.g., handle_compaction sending '/compact', handle_usage_limit sending '/refresh') bypasses:
  - the operator's safety.require_prompt_active=true policy,
  - every [[safety.rules]] custom gate,
  - the [safety.command_gate] allow/deny list.

Site 2 (main.rs:20511, RobotCommands::Workflow::Abort): identical construction. abort_execution_with_cx runs compensation steps through the same injector; any send_text in a compensation path bypasses the same three surfaces.

Compare the correctly-gated paths:
  - RobotCommands::Send at main.rs:17652 uses config.safety.require_prompt_active AND .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules.
  - Non-robot workflow invocations at main.rs:21361, 21533, 25051, 27347 all wire the full policy surface.

NOT a duplicate of ft-48ah6 (read-only policy): this bead is specifically about the write/mutation surface (workflow's send_text / command-bearing steps). ft-48ah6 covered the read/search side.

Fix: replace the hardcoded 'false' with config.safety.require_prompt_active at both sites, and chain .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()) before passing the engine to PolicyGatedInjector. Rate-limit args are already correct.

Regression test: a send inside a robot-invoked workflow must be DENIED when config.safety.require_prompt_active=true AND no prompt is active, matching the direct-send gate behavior. Currently it is allowed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:46:59.925741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084786Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084480Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 834b59f2. Both RobotCommands::Workflow::Run (main.rs:19831) and ::Abort (main.rs:20520) now build PolicyEngine with config.safety.require_prompt_active and chain .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules — matching the construction at RobotCommands::Send (main.rs:17652) and non-robot workflow sites (21361/21533/25051/27347). Regression test robot_workflow_policy_engine_denies_send_when_prompt_required_ft_4z7vh asserts SendText is denied when require_prompt_active=true + command_running. Test binary compile is currently blocked by pre-existing concurrent-agent breakage (E0603 macro select private, E0063 missing defer_fts_triggers field) unrelated to this fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-55l4f","title":"[review][MEDIUM] wa_wait_for.json golden freezes polls:1 — may flake under high-concurrency CI load","description":"In crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_wait_for.json (landed in ecae6caa):

    "polls": 1

The canonicalize fn in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs only zeroes `now | elapsed_ms | captured_at | *_ms | score | semantic_score`. The `polls` field is not canonicalized.

Under load, this can flake:
- Each wa.wait_for poll spawns a fresh python3 subprocess (fake-wezterm.py) via tokio::Command in crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:2142-2155.
- On first-poll cache-cold with the swarm running 20+ agents hammering CPU/disk, the subprocess spawn + file read can be slow enough that the poll scheduler issues a second poll before the first returns matching text (build ready matches ready$ immediately on content).
- polls jumps to 2+, golden is polls==1, test fails.

Repro likelihood: low under bench, moderate-to-high under concurrent cargo test + rch + other agent builds hammering I/O.

Fix options (pick one):
1. Canonicalize `polls` to 0 in canonicalize() alongside elapsed_ms/now/captured_at. Pro: stable golden. Con: loses a genuine signal — golden no longer catches regressions where wait_for suddenly needs 10 polls for a match it used to catch on the first try.
2. Split `polls` out of the golden capture: keep it in success_envelope but canonicalize to "polls >= 1" assertion in assert_wait_for_success_data (already there at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:~590 — `polls >= 1`), then canonicalize polls to 0 so the golden only sees the structural presence.
3. Accept the flake risk and document that re-running tests clears transient failures.

Option 2 is cleanest: the semantic "polls >= 1" invariant stays enforced; the byte-stable golden covers structure.

Severity: MEDIUM. Not a correctness bug; CI flake risk. Discovered during reviewer audit round 7, 2026-04-23 when verifying ecae6caa (ft-qyw1z+ft-4o5mb CRITICAL fix).

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, flake.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:30.203619Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:46:07.148812Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

 {"id":"ft-5m5xc","title":"scan_pipeline: enable_ansi_analysis config field is dead — never read, setting false has zero effect","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:40-62 ScanPipelineConfig declares 'pub enable_ansi_analysis: bool' with Default = true, but no code in the crate ever reads this field. process() at :405 and process_chunk() at :443 unconditionally call scan_newlines_and_ansi* regardless of the flag. An operator configuring 'enable_ansi_analysis = false' in their config expecting to disable the ANSI byte counting gets no behavior change. This is a reality-gap defect: the documented config surface lies about what it controls. Fix: gate the ansi-scan call on self.config.enable_ansi_analysis; when false, skip the scan and return a ScanMetricsSummary with ansi_byte_count=0 and ansi_density=0 (preserving the newline_count + logical_lines numbers that don't depend on ANSI scanning). Default stays true so existing callers see no change. Regression test: set enable_ansi_analysis=false, feed input containing ESC bytes, assert ansi_byte_count==0 in returned ScanOutput.metrics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:47:35.328044Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053447Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3a4dfcd8 (fix(scan_pipeline): honor enable_ansi_analysis flag in batch + chunked paths). Two regression tests pin batch (process) + chunked (process_chunk) contracts, including a sanity check that the default config still counts ANSI bytes. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-o657v","title":"conformance_mcp_manifest: pin tool input_schema additionalProperties:false invariant","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/conformance_mcp_manifest.rs` rejects unknown top-level fields (via `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`), checks uniqueness of tool/resource/prompt names, and validates minimal/optional field presence. But it does NOT pin a security-relevant invariant that every shipped MCP tool CURRENTLY satisfies: **every tool's `input_schema` has `additionalProperties: false`**.

Verified by:

    $ rg -c 'additionalProperties' crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs
    29
    $ rg -c 'input_schema:' crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs
    29

All 29 input_schemas currently set it. Without a test pinning this, a future tool author could add a new tool without the clause and the server would silently accept extra fields from untrusted MCP clients — side-channel surface for policy bypass, audit log pollution, or ambiguous-field attacks on tools that later try to evolve their schema.

# Fix

Add `every_tool_input_schema_denies_additional_properties` in conformance_mcp_manifest.rs that iterates `server.tools()` from `build_server_with_db(&Config::default(), Some(...))` and asserts each `tool.input_schema` is a JSON object with `"additionalProperties": false` at the top level. If a tool's schema is NOT type=object (e.g. `{}`, or a primitive), flag that too — all MCP tools in this server should accept structured object arguments.

# Non-fix

Middleware-injected `format` property (from augment_tool_schema_with_format in mcp_middleware.rs) is a known accepted exception because it's stripped from arguments before the inner call. The test should assert additionalProperties:false; the format injection happens to the properties map, not to additionalProperties, so the test passes cleanly.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no live defect; every current tool sets the bit. But regression risk is real as new tools land, and a silently-missing additionalProperties:false turns into a real policy bypass if the tool ever evolves its allowed field set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:03:57.448008Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:52.683351Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:52.683105Z","close_reason":"Landed as part of f73e139a (absorbed commit). every_tool_input_schema_denies_additional_properties iterates server.tools() from build_server_with_db + asserts each input_schema is type=object with additionalProperties literal false — pins the 29/29 invariant so future tools without the clause break CI instead of silently widening the accepted field set.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-odywh","title":"mux local spawn offload regression coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T04:24:40.694101Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:30:56.950244Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:30:56.949848Z","close_reason":"Verified in f8e260d7","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-ojgmo","title":"[deadlock] workflow runner cx-cancel can strand on idle event subscriber","description":"File: crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:769-790; crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs:805-810,2157-2170; crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs:2159-2167

Repro narrative:
1. Start WorkflowRunner::run_with_cx(...) on an idle EventBus with no detection events flowing.
2. Let the loop pass the top-of-loop cx.checkpoint() and park inside subscriber.recv_cx(cx).
3. Cancel the caller Cx after the recv is already suspended.
4. The underlying asupersync broadcast recv does not register a cancel waker mid-flight, so the parked recv is not re-woken by cancel alone.
5. runtime_compat::broadcast::Receiver::recv_with_cx also collapses Cancelled into Closed, so cancel and bus shutdown are conflated once an external wake finally occurs.
6. Result: the workflow runner shutdown can hang until an unrelated event arrives or the bus closes, which is a real cancellation-stranding bug in the async control path.

Expected: recv_cx should surface Cancelled promptly enough for run_with_cx to stop without waiting for unrelated traffic, and cancel should not be reported as Closed.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:38.816705Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:44.018595Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-oku4q","title":"mux dispatch swallows readable wait failures","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:18:25.203996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:25:31.725708Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:25:31.725114Z","close_reason":"Landed in 1ad5c8cc (fix: connector_outbound_bridge zero-capacity, storage FTS rename, mux dispatch error propagation)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-olq2e","title":"[HIGH] integration_mux_checkpoint_topology never validates post-rollback topology","description":"Audit finding from commit 909c3d89.

File: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs:385-389

The test claims to verify that rollback restores the original topology, but it reconstructs `restored_topo` from `initial_topo_json` captured before mutation, not from the server/registry state after rollback:

- line 308 serializes the initial topology into `initial_topo_json`
- lines 385-389 deserialize that same baseline JSON and compare it to `initial_topo`

That assertion is vacuous: it will pass even if `RemoteRequest::Rollback` leaves the server in the wrong pane/tree layout, as long as the old serialized baseline is still parseable. The test currently proves JSON roundtrip of the baseline snapshot, not rollback correctness.

Expected fix:
- derive the post-rollback topology from the actual restored server state (or its durable checkpoint payload), then compare pane ids/tree structure against the pre-expansion baseline
- keep the current rollback-history assertions as secondary checks, not the only evidence

Regression risk: high false confidence on mux rollback behavior in the persistence lane.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:49:43.010557Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:20.471789Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:20.471571Z","close_reason":"Phase 5 now derives post-rollback pane id set from server.registry().snapshot() instead of re-deserializing the pre-mutation baseline JSON. Primary assertion compares the live post-rollback pane id set against pre-expansion baseline; baseline JSON roundtrip retained as secondary serde check. All 3 tests in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs pass. Fix landed in 30172171.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mux","review","session-persistence","test-gap"]}

 {"id":"ft-t4n0z","title":"[fuzz] add termwiz::input::InputParser::parse fuzz target for raw keystroke bytes","description":"Surface: frankenterm/termwiz/src/input.rs:1454 — pub fn parse(&mut self, bytes: &[u8], callback, maybe_more). Parses raw keystroke ANSI escape sequences (arrow keys, function keys, mouse events, CSI u keys, bracketed paste, OSC responses) from stdin. Directly accepts untrusted byte streams via &[u8]; no existing fuzz target covers it (termwiz_csi_parser is a different stage — output CSI — and escape_parser_raw covers terminal output parsing, not keyboard input).

Proposed harness: fuzz/fuzz_targets/termwiz_input_parser.rs
  #![no_main]
  use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
  use termwiz::input::InputParser;
  fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
      if data.len() > 1_000_000 { return; }
      let mut parser = InputParser::new();
      let _ = parser.parse_as_vec(data, false);
      let _ = parser.parse_as_vec(data, true);  // maybe_more=true path
  });

Corpus seeds: fuzz/corpus/termwiz_input_parser/ — minimal valid sequences from existing termwiz tests (input.rs:1482-1540 fixtures): empty, bare ESC, CSI cursor keys, CSI u keyboard, SS3 function keys, OSC 52 clipboard response, bracketed paste markers, bracket mouse reports (SGR and legacy).

Add to fuzz/Cargo.toml [[bin]] with the termwiz path dependency already present.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:04.832273Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:00:04.935376Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:00:04.934979Z","close_reason":"Added fuzz/fuzz_targets/termwiz_input_parser.rs + 25 corpus seeds; cargo fuzz build termwiz_input_parser succeeds","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-t62hq","title":"[SEV] mcp wa.wait_for + wa.send accept timeout_secs=0 despite schema minimum:1 — silent zero-duration wait","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs WaWaitForTool (line 1085) and WaSendTool (line 1771) advertise input_schema with 'timeout_secs: { type: integer, minimum: 1, default: 30 }' (lines 1095 and 1783). WaitForParams.timeout_secs and SendParams.timeout_secs are both u64 in mcp_types.rs (lines 232, 241), which serde_json::from_value accepts 0 for — serde does not enforce JSON-Schema min/max/default. No post-parse bounds check exists at either call site.

Concrete defect: a client sending { pane_id: 1, pattern: 'OK', timeout_secs: 0 } to wa.wait_for hits line 1166 'let timeout = std::time::Duration::from_secs(timeout_secs);' with timeout = 0. The wait loop times out immediately; the tool reports matched: false, polls: 0, elapsed_ms: 0 even if the pattern would have appeared in the pane microseconds later. Same gap in wa.send at line 1928. Clients that type-coerce (e.g., JS Math.floor(0.5) = 0, CLI timeout=0 as 'no timeout') land on this silently.

Precedent: the wa.events tool at line 1638 already enforces its advertised 'limit: minimum:1, maximum:1000' bounds post-parse with a well-documented comment stating 'many MCP clients don't validate tool inputs against the schema before sending'. That comment applies equally to timeout_secs.

Fix: add the same post-parse validation for timeout_secs at both call sites, returning MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with a message pointing at the schema's minimum. Pattern matches the wa.events enforcement block exactly.

Also note (tracked separately if filed): wa.cass_search, wa.cass_view, wa.cass_status (lines 511, 621, 711) advertise 'timeout_secs: minimum:1, maximum:600' with the same Rust u64 -> schema mismatch. They pass params.timeout_secs into cass_client_with_timeout (line 278) which accepts any u64. This bead scopes to wa.wait_for + wa.send only to keep the fix focused.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:25:59.494378Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:29:38.622911Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:29:38.622531Z","close_reason":"Completed: added post-parse timeout_secs>=1 validation at WaWaitForTool.call and WaSendTool.call matching the wa.events limit-enforcement pattern (line 1638). Clients sending timeout_secs=0 now receive MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS with a schema-referencing hint instead of a silent zero-duration no-op. cass tools (lines 511/621/711) have the same gap, scoped out. Commit 8dc7dbde.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-t995m","title":"escape-parser: tmux_cc feature build broken (130 compile errors) — proptest macro expansion E0277 on Vec<usize>/String strategies","description":"cargo test -p frankenterm-escape-parser --lib --features tmux_cc fails with 130 compile errors. All from the existing proptest block at tmux_cc/mod.rs:1397-1409 (parser_events_are_chunk_boundary_invariant, added by 3abc11d2). Error shape: E0277 'the size for values of type [usize] cannot be known at compilation time' inside proptest::sugar::NamedArguments<N, V> — the macro is trying to use slice-unsized V where it should be Vec-sized. Also 'str' / '[usize]' sized errors with the same root cause. Looks like a proptest 1.11 macro-expansion breakage or a version mismatch between the workspace pin (1.11) and the crate's dev-dep pin (1.8). The tmux_cc feature tests never run in CI when this is broken — my ft-kfy99 fix is latent until this unblocks. Scope: this bead only tracks the test-build breakage; the underlying logic change in ft-kfy99 is unaffected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:05.738611Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:22:12.427122Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:22:12.426873Z","close_reason":"Landed in 03bfed4c (fix(escape-parser): tmux_cc feature requires std). Cargo.toml tmux_cc feature now chains 'std' so the module's HashMap/String/ToString/format! usage resolves. cargo check -p frankenterm-escape-parser --tests --features tmux_cc now builds clean (3.86s); pre-fix it produced 90 compile errors. Unblocks ft-kfy99's regression test + parser_events_are_chunk_boundary_invariant.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-tczj7","title":"[review][LOW] ecae6caa rewrite drops FT-MCP-0001 tool-level serde-reject coverage, replaces with framework InvalidParams only","description":"The pre-commit mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (ft-4o5mb first pass) exercised the TOOL-level serde rejection path: call_tool with wrong-typed args like `{"query": 42}` would reach the tool handler at crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:1387-1399 where `serde_json::from_value::<SearchParams>` failed and emitted the documented MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS (aka FT-MCP-0001) envelope.

ecae6caa replaced that with `{"query": "needle", "limit": 0}` — which triggers FRAMEWORK-level schema validation (limit minimum:1) and returns `FrameworkMcpError { code: InvalidParams }`, serialized by parse_invalid_args_response as `{"kind": "framework_error", "code": "InvalidParams", ...}`. The 4 tests now assert `assert_framework_invalid_params_response(...)` with the framework error shape, NOT the tool envelope shape.

Effect: the FT-MCP-0001 tool-level error contract is no longer fenced by any test in this file. A refactor that deletes or renames the serde-reject branch at mcp_tools.rs:1387-1399, 1392-1396, etc. — or that changes the hint strings quoted in the original bead ft-4o5mb description — passes these tests silently.

Scope question: is FT-MCP-0001 still meant to be part of the public MCP contract, or has the framework schema validation now superseded it? If superseded, mcp_tools.rs:1387-1399 and the parallel branches in wa.get_text/wa.send/wa.wait_for are dead code and should be removed. If not superseded, we need one regression test per tool that slips past schema validation into the serde-reject path.

Easiest slip path: a field whose JSON-schema type is `integer` accepts `0` through bounds validation but fails at serde deserialization because the tool struct uses `NonZeroU64`. Not the case today (tool structs use plain u64), so the only reliable way to hit serde rejection is with wrong-typed args — which framework may or may not catch depending on schema strictness for each tool.

Fix path: one regression test per tool sending args that pass schema validation but fail serde. Concrete candidate: send an array as a string field, or malform a nested object that additionalProperties:false doesn't check recursively. Verify FT-MCP-0001 still surfaces.

Severity: LOW — not a regression in production behavior, just a coverage regression. Documented so a future ft-4o5mb-follow-up bead can pick it up.

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, coverage. Audit round 7, 2026-04-23, verifying ecae6caa.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:47.415916Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:47.415916Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

 {"id":"ft-trby3","title":"[reality-gap] RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite is enum-reachable but runtime-unreachable","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:62 lists "**Replay & Forensics**: Capture, replay, and
diff decision graphs for post-incident analysis and regression
testing" as a core feature. No distinction between backends is
offered — the user's mental model is "replay just works."

# Reality (code)

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs:560-578`:

```rust
match config.backend {
    RecorderBackendKind::AppendLog => { ...opens storage... }
    RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite => Err(
        RecorderStorageError::BackendUnavailable {
            backend: RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite,
            message: "frankensqlite backend not yet implemented".to_string(),
        }
    ),
}
```

The enum variant is public, serde-deserializable (confirmed by
presence on `RecorderStorageConfig.backend` field), and reachable
from any operator who writes `backend = "frankensqlite"` (or
whatever serde rename applies) in their ft.toml's recorder section.

# Failure shape

Operator configures frankensqlite → app bootstraps → recorder
bootstrap returns `BackendUnavailable` → app either:
  (a) aborts with the "not yet implemented" message (informative);
  (b) silently falls back to AppendLog (if caller does);
  (c) propagates the error and the whole watch daemon fails to start.

Which branch fires depends on callers of `bootstrap_recorder_storage`
— worth verifying in a follow-up. Best case (a) wastes operator's
time; worst case (c) is a surprise crash.

# Recommended disposition

Either:
  (a) Remove `FrankenSqlite` from the serde-exposed enum until the
      backend is actually implemented. Zero blast radius — the
      variant is only reachable by operator opt-in today.
  (b) Keep the variant but add a deprecation/preview flag that
      operators must explicitly set (e.g., `experimental_backend =
      true`) so accidental selection is impossible.
  (c) Implement the FrankenSqlite backend.

(a) or (b) ship in minutes; (c) is a subsystem-sized undertaking.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:30.571102Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:55.998924Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:51:20.514877Z","close_reason":"README + flight-recorder docs now disclose that append_log is the only shipped recorder backend and frankensqlite remains rollout/test-only until bootstrap support lands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reality-gap","recorder","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-trot7","title":"telemetry::Histogram::record poisons total_sum/min/max on f64::INFINITY (NaN guard is too narrow)","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs:339\` — \`Histogram::record\`
guards against NaN but does **not** guard against \`f64::INFINITY\` or
\`f64::NEG_INFINITY\`. The recently-added [ft-b4l62] guard catches NaN
because NaN poisons \`partial_cmp\` and the running sum, but Inf has the
same poisoning shape and slips through unflagged.

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller invokes \`Histogram::record(f64::INFINITY)\`. Easy upstream
   trigger: any latency calculation of the form
   \`numerator / denom_seconds\` where \`denom_seconds == 0.0\` (clock
   skew, mocked time fixture, division-before-clamp bug). Or a
   subtraction of two timestamps that overflowed to Inf in a longer
   pipeline.
2. Line 340 \`if value.is_nan() { return; }\` — Inf is not NaN, falls
   through to mutate state.
3. Line 344 \`self.total_sum += value\`. \`f64 + INF = INF\`. \`total_sum\`
   is now \`INF\` permanently — every subsequent finite sample preserves
   the INF (\`INF + finite = INF\`).
4. Line 348-349 \`if value > self.max { self.max = value }\`. Initial
   \`max = NEG_INFINITY\` so \`INF > NEG_INFINITY\` is true → \`max = INF\`.
   Every later finite value compares \`finite > INF\` → false, so \`max\`
   stays at INF forever.
5. \`Histogram::mean()\` (line 401) returns \`INF / count = INF\`.
6. \`Histogram::summary()\` returns \`Some(INF)\` for \`mean\`, \`max\`, and
   potentially \`p99\`. \`HistogramSummary\` is \`Serialize\`; serde_json's
   default serializer emits \`null\` for non-finite f64 (RFC 8259 forbids
   Inf in JSON), silently dropping the value from telemetry payloads
   that downstream dashboards consume.
7. Symmetric for \`record(f64::NEG_INFINITY)\` — \`total_sum\` and \`min\`
   become NEG_INFINITY permanently, mean reports \`-INF\`.

## Severity rationale

- Same poisoning shape as ft-b4l62 (NaN), ft-761tz (NaN in
  disk_pressure), ft-yskcu (NaN in continuous_backpressure), ft-7s0vw
  (overflow in event_id) — silent wrong answer with no self-recovery.
- Existing NaN guard makes the absence of an Inf guard surprising:
  reviewers reading the code see \`is_nan()\` and assume the histogram
  is hardened, when it is not.
- Reachable via legitimate division-by-zero in any client computing
  durations or rates as f64.

## Fix sketch

1. \`Histogram::record\` (line 340) — broaden the guard from
   \`if value.is_nan()\` to \`if !value.is_finite()\`. \`f64::is_finite\`
   is true iff the value is neither NaN nor ±Infinity.
2. Update the doc comment and \`[ft-b4l62]\` reference to note that the
   guard now covers Inf as well.
3. Regression tests covering: (a) record(INFINITY) is no-op,
   total_sum stays 0.0, mean stays None; (b) record(NEG_INFINITY) is
   no-op; (c) record(finite) then record(INFINITY) then record(finite)
   — second finite blends correctly without Inf contamination;
   (d) summary().max stays Some(finite_value) after an Inf attempt.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs\` only. No cross-crate API
changes. \`HistogramSummary\` shape unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:58:02.402142Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:14.413298Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:14.413032Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit f73e139a: Histogram::record now uses !value.is_finite() to reject both NaN and ±INFINITY. 4 regression tests added covering pos-Inf, neg-Inf, all-Inf, and summary().max stays finite after Inf attempt. All 74 histogram-related tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target. Pattern matches the arithmetic-poison family alongside ft-b4l62 (NaN guard, original), ft-761tz, ft-yskcu, ft-7s0vw.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
